failures.claim: target: { value: 2 },   // opponent (primitive number wrapped in { value })
failures.expected: The target selection value is a choice object { value: 2, display: '...' }; { value: 2 } matcher matches it via general field equality (last branch of selectionMatchesValue), not via the primitive { value } branch
failures.actual: Comment says 'primitive number wrapped in { value }', but selectionMatchesValue only enters the primitive branch when typeof value !== 'object'; a choice object is an object, so field equality applies. Section 1.3 of the same doc correctly says '{ value, display } choice object'.

failures.claim: Supply a custom narrator for games with rich arg types where the default JSON formatting reads poorly
failures.expected: Default narrator uses describeMoveDestination(args), which explicitly avoids JSON; custom narrators are needed when describeMoveDestination cannot extract a readable destination string
failures.actual: src/session/game-session.ts line 1213 comment: 'Formats a readable destination from destination-like args only (never raw element ids), so the narration reads as prose instead of dumping JSON.' The default does NOT use JSON formatting; the doc inverts the causality.
